meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no version control (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Project Template for Version Control
content.seo_description: Stop losing work. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to master version control and build professional-grade software.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template eliminates version control chaos by automating repository initialization, branch management, and commit hygiene. By pairing this with Claude Code, you transform manual tracking into an autonomous workflow, ensuring your code remains clean, revertible, and professional—the exact standard required for high-impact engineering portfolios.
content.intro:
You’ve spent hours perfecting a feature, only to overwrite a crucial file or lose your progress in a sea of 'final_v2_new' folders. This isn't just a minor setback; it is the silent project killer that separates hobbyists from professional software engineers. Without a robust version control strategy, your best work is perpetually at risk of vanishing into the void.

But the tide is turning. By leveraging the Antigravity project template, you can hard-wire professional infrastructure into your workflow from day one. Combined with the raw power of Claude Code, you aren't just writing code—you are architecting a resilient development environment that protects your progress and scales with your ambition.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish a professional root directory that enforces strict naming conventions and structured versioning from the moment you start.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project scaffold using the Antigravity standard template, ensuring Git-ready structure, .gitignore for modern stacks, and README documentation placeholders.

steps.title: Configure Automated Versioning
steps.description: Set up hooks that prevent you from pushing uncommitted changes or broken builds to your main branch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the project environment to include pre-commit hooks that validate code formatting and run basic linting before any commit is finalized.

steps.title: Implement Branching Logic
steps.description: Learn to isolate your experimental features from your stable codebase using the Antigravity branching workflow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a new feature branch and generate a template for the feature description, ensuring my commit messages align with conventional commit standards.

steps.title: Sync with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to monitor your directory changes and proactively suggest commits when significant milestones are reached.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Monitor the current directory for changes and propose a commit message that follows the semantic versioning schema whenever a functional block is completed.

steps.title: Finalize and Merge
steps.description: Execute a clean merge of your feature branch, ensuring the project history remains readable and professional for future employers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current feature branch against the main branch, identify potential merge conflicts, and prepare a squashed commit for the final merge.

pro_tips: Always use atomic commits; one feature per commit makes reverting catastrophic errors painless.
Treat your README.md as a living document; update it via Claude Code every time you change a major dependency.
Use Git tags for every milestone; it turns your GitHub profile into a chronological roadmap of your growth.
Never commit API keys or environment variables; ensure your Antigravity template includes a strict .env.example file.
Practice 'Clean History' by rebasing your feature branches regularly to keep your project timeline linear and readable.
content.next_steps: Now that your environment is secured, deploy your project to a cloud host like Vercel or Railway and link your repository to your portfolio site to showcase your professional workflow to recruiters.
content.conversion_block: Stop settling for messy code and lost progress. Master the tools that the top 1% of AI engineers use to ship software at velocity. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
